findings.summary: CONFIRMED real UX defect + fix spec. Agents-screen per-agent 'status' (agents.py:1794: `if run['status']=='failed': state='error'`) is computed from the SINGLE most-recent AgentRun with NO transient/refund classification and NO window. A TRANSIENT/REFUNDED hard-failure — e.g. tailor run 'The AI service is temporarily unavailable' (LLMUnavailable, refunded), or a rate-limited 429/refunded sweep run — sets status='failed' and paints the agent card RED until the next run, even though the agent is healthy and the user was refunded. This is what the operator saw as 'some agents erroring out'. Graceful degrades (status='completed' + coverLetterUnavailable) already correctly show 'active'. FIX: a transient/upstream-unavailable OR refunded (costUsd NULL) last-run failure must NOT render a persistent 'error' badge (show 'active', optional subtle degraded hint); a GENUINE billed agent-logic failure still shows 'error'; AND a persistence guard — last N(≈3) runs ALL failed → 'error' so real breakage still surfaces. Fields available on AgentRun: status, error(msg), costUsd(NULL=refund), output(flags), billingAuditJson.
reproduction: Load /dashboard/agents on prod → some agents show error state / erroring runs (operator report)
findings.expected: Agents run cleanly OR degrade honestly (no unhandled 500/Traceback)
findings.observed: Operator sees agents 'erroring out' (specifics under RCA)
findings.verifiedBy: qa-adversary a4bd416d PROD-VERIFY-ef7df30.md — PASS /agents/catalog vs /agents 0 error/0 failed/0 disagreements across 8 agents; OBS-A/B live-correct
findings.rca: /agents/catalog status = last-run-only. resumeTailoring red badge = tail artifact of orchestrator's RUN-SWEEP.md 429 (refunded). No unhandled 500/Traceback on any agent-run path; no cross-provider silent substitution (billingAuditJson+refund verified).
findings.reviewFail: Fix af98b04 FAILED adversarial review (ERR-001-REVIEW.md, reviewer addd9666). BLOCKER: _is_transient_or_refunded_failure short-circuits True on costUsd IS NULL, but EVERY failed run has costUsd=None (no finish(...,'failed',...) call site records cost — only the success path at agents.py:841 does). So the refund heuristic matches ALL failures, the keyword list is dead code, and a genuine billed logic error (KeyError/validation) is misclassified transient → status 'active', HIDING a broken agent (proven live: 50% genuine-failure rate never trips the 3-in-a-row chronic guard → 'active' forever). The guard test passed only because it seeded an unrealistic cost_usd=0.0164 on a FAILED run. REWORK: drop the costUsd heuristic; classify transient by ERROR-MESSAGE keyword only (LLMUnavailable msg 'temporarily unavailable' + upstream 429/503/502/504/timeout/overloaded/rate-limit); keep chronic guard; CORRECT the test to production-realistic seeding (failed runs -> costUsd=None).

findings.summary: RESOLVED — NOT A DEFECT. Live probe (LIVE-CATALOG-STATUS-NOW.md) + code trace (COVER-STATUS-CONFIG-SCOUT.md) + grep confirm: (1) NO hardcoded claude-3-haiku anywhere in apps/api/app; deployed tier default is AETHER_MODEL_REASONING=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro. (2) The claude-3-haiku that returned empty JSON at 23:36 was the OPERATOR'S OWN per-agent model selection for coverLetter at that time; the system honored the choice, got empty content, retried, then honestly degraded (coverLetterUnavailable, \$0 refunded) — the correct no-silent-substitution behavior. (3) Operator has since switched coverLetter to anthropic/claude-sonnet-5, which runs successfully (\$0.0016 billed). Honoring a user-selected weak model + honest refund on failure is working as designed; changing it would violate the no-substitution mandate. No code change.
reproduction: Run coverLetter repeatedly → intermittent empty-content from claude-3-haiku → honest coverLetterUnavailable degrade
